description: Three photos of a healthy bermuda lawn at peak summer growth in Atlanta. Tests that the AI provides maintenance-only recommendations and does not invent disease or pest issues.

photos.caption: Wide-angle view of dense, uniformly green bermuda turf at 1.5-inch mowing height, no bare spots.
Close-up of bermuda leaf blades showing healthy dark-green color and fine texture.
Side-angle view showing uniform canopy density and consistent mowing height across the yard.

profile.notes: Front yard, mowed at 1.5 inches every 5 days, no problems noted. Lawn looks great this season.
profile.currentRoutine: Mow every 5 days at 1.5 inches, irrigate 3x per week totaling 1 inch, applied 28-0-3 fertilizer in June.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Three views of a dense, uniformly green bermuda lawn at peak summer growth. No signs of disease, pest damage, drought stress, weeds, or bare spots. Canopy is consistent and well-manicured.
